title: Devices are not mounted inside rootless container and groups are not properly ported
body:
### Issue Description

I can't mount devices inside a podman container when rootless. I tried the same quadlet file with rootful and I'm able to use mounted devices.

I'm using quadlet and this is the content of the file:

```
[Unit]
Description=Tdarr container
After=local-fs.target

[Container]
Image=ghcr.io/haveagitgat/tdarr:latest
ContainerName=tdarr
AutoUpdate=registry
Environment=PUID=1002
Environment=PGID=1002
Environment=UMASK=002
Environment=TZ=Europe/Amsterdam
Environment=serverIP=0.0.0.0
Environment=serverPort=8266
Environment=webUIPort=8265
Environment=internalNode=true
Environment=inContainer=true
Environment=ffmpegVersion=6
Environment=nodeName=MyInternalNode
PublishPort=8265:8265
PublishPort=8266:8266
Volume=${CONFIG_DIR}/tdarr/server:/app/server:Z
Volume=${CONFIG_DIR}/tdarr/configs:/app/configs:Z
Volume=${CONFIG_DIR}/tdarr/logs:/app/logs:Z
Volume=${CONFIG_DIR}/tdarr/transcode_cache:/temp:Z
Volume=${DATA_DIR}/test:/media:z
AddDevice=/dev/dri:/dev/dri
#Network=pqsw.network
User=0
UserNS=keep-id
GroupAdd=keep-groups
HealthStartPeriod=1m
HealthCmd=CMD-SHELL curl -f --insecure http://localhost:8265 || exit 1
HealthInterval=30s
HealthRetries=2
HealthOnFailure=kill

[Service]
Restart=on-failure

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

User `podman` is member of groups `render` and `video`:
```
podman@host~> groups
podman video render
```

And this are the devices on the host:

```
podman@host:~> ls -l /dev/dri
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root         80 Jan 11 11:26 by-path
crw-rw----. 1 root video  226,   1 Jan 11 11:26 card1
crw-rw----. 1 root render 226, 128 Jan 11 11:26 renderD128
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Add user to `video` and `render` group
2. Lauch rootles container with `GroupAdd=keep-groups` directive
3. Run `podman inspect tdarr` and see no devices are mounted


### Describe the results you received

When executing `podman inspect tdarr` I don't see any device:
```
...
"CpusetMems": "",
"Devices": [],
"DiskQuota": 0,
...
```
Moreover, when inspecting the container, I don't see the groups `video` and `render` begin ported inside the container:

```
podman@host:~> podman exec -it tdarr bash
root@afbf943fadda:/# cd /dev/dri/
root@afbf943fadda:/dev/dri# ls -l
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 nobody nogroup       80 Jan 11 11:26 by-path
crw-rw----. 1 nobody nogroup 226,   1 Jan 11 11:26 card1
crw-rw----. 1 nobody nogroup 226, 128 Jan 11 11:26 renderD128
```

This is how it looks on the host:

```
podman@host:~> cd /dev/dri
podman@host:/dev/dri> ls -l
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root         80 Jan 11 11:26 by-path
crw-rw----. 1 root video  226,   1 Jan 11 11:26 card1
crw-rw----. 1 root render 226, 128 Jan 11 11:26 renderD128
```


### Describe the results you expected

I would expect to have groups `video` and `render` inside the container.
I would expect to see devices present when running `podman inspect tdarr`

When rootful, it looks like this:
```
"Devices": [
                    {
                         "PathOnHost": "/dev/dri/card1",
                         "PathInContainer": "/dev/dri/card1",
                         "CgroupPermissions": ""
                    },
                    {
                         "PathOnHost": "/dev/dri/renderD128",
                         "PathInContainer": "/dev/dri/renderD128",
                         "CgroupPermissions": ""
                    }
               ],
```

### podman info output

```yaml

  cgroupManager: systemd

      host_id: 1002

    - container_id: 0

    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 231072
      size: 65536
  kernel: 6.12.8-2-default
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
> I would expect to have groups `video` and `render` inside the container.
> I would expect to see devices present when running `podman inspect tdarr`

In an unprivileged user namespace, host GIDs cannot be mapped, unless the user has access to them (i.e. added to /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid).  Any files or processes with unmapped GIDs will appear as owned by nobody:nogroup within the namespace.

If your user had access to the devices on the host and the access was granted through groups membership, then it should still have it inside the user namespace with keep-groups.

Can the user access the devices?

Same for me, previously it worked, but nowadays, since 1 or 2 weeks, my groups are not mapped even if I specify the keep-groups option. My user is member of 4 groups on the host, but has only one group in the container. Also the desired group (render) isn't in the /etc/subgid file. I have podman v4.9.3 on Ubuntu 24.04.

I'm curious why did it work earlier. Isn't there a way to use GPU devices in rootless containers?

UPDATE: I've solved it. In my configuration I also set the User and Group ids additionaly to the UserNS and AddGroup settings, when I removed the User and Group configs, the keep-groups setting worked and my user could access the gpu devices.

@Breinich do you mind sharing your quadlet file?
I'm getting crazy and I still can't get the iGPU to work with a rootless container.

@dozenposture sure, here it is:

```
[Unit]
Description=Jellyfin media service
RequiresMountsFor=/mnt/data

[Container]
Image=docker.io/jellyfin/jellyfin:latest
AutoUpdate=registry
UserNS=keep-id
PodmanArgs=--group-add keep-groups
#GroupAdd=keep-groups
PublishPort=8096:8096/tcp
AddDevice=/dev/dri/:/dev/dri/
Volume=/.../config:/config
Volume=/.../cache:/cache
Volume=/mnt/data/media:/data/media
Timezone=Europe/Budapest

[Service]
# Inform systemd of additional exit status
SuccessExitStatus=0 143
Restart=always

[Install]
# Start by default on boot
WantedBy=default.target
```

**Also note, that the user, who runs this container, is member of the video and render groups, this is essential.**

And also note that the groups inside the container won't have the original name of render and video, instead something else, so you should not check if the device is available via only looking at the group names.

Hi @Breinich do you know why you had to use podman args instead of the GroupAdd=keep-groups? This fixed it for me (I was trying to use GroupAdd=keep-groups) but it seems weird. I tried searching if this was added recently and that maybe my podman version is too old but no luck. I appreciate your help figuring out my issue (now I can remove global access to the renderer) but now it leaves me wondering why

EDIT: found my answer just manually searching release notes finally found it was added in 5.1 and I'm still on 4.9 https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.1.0
If you're on Ubuntu, a dist-upgrade could solve your issue with that, on 25.04 the 5.4 version of Podman is available.

I have come across this myself and have not found a solution. I was seeing it in a Jellyfin container as above but I have put together a more minimal example using plain Podman, without the systemd integration, and avoiding Jellyfin for now.

I am running a BeeLink EQ14 ([Intel N150](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/sku/241636/intel-processor-n150-6m-cache-up-to-3-60-ghz/specifications.html)) with:
- Podman version: 5.6.0
- OS: openSUSE MicroOS Tumbleweed
- Kernel: 6.16.3-1-default
- SELinux disabled with `enforcing=0`

# Working on host

On the system I have the device showing up as:
```console
$ ls -hl /dev/dri/
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root         80 Sep  6 00:53 by-path
crw-rw----. 1 root video  226,   1 Sep  6 00:53 card1
crw-rw----. 1 root render 226, 128 Sep  6 00:53 renderD128
```
and with a user `myuser` in the `render` group (as a secondary):
```console
$ id
uid=1001(myuser) gid=1001(mygroup) groups=1001(mygroup),485(video),488(render) context=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023
```
I can use the `vainfo` tool to query the device:
```console
$ vainfo --display drm
Trying display: drm
libva info: VA-API version 1.22.0
libva info: Trying to open /usr/lib64/dri/iHD_drv_video.so
libva info: Found init function __vaDriverInit_1_22
libva info: va_openDriver() returns 0
vainfo: VA-API version: 1.22 (libva 2.22.0)
vainfo: Driver version: Intel iHD driver for Intel(R) Gen Graphics - 25.2.6 ()
vainfo: Supported profile and entrypoints
      VAProfileNone                   : VAEntrypointVideoProc
      VAProfileNone                   : VAEntrypointStats
      ...
```

# Failing on rootless Podman

I put together a simple Containerfile to test it (note the `CMD` is `vainfo --display drm`):
```Dockerfile
FROM alpine:3.22
RUN echo https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.22/community/ >> /etc/apk/repositories
RUN apk add libva-utils intel-media-driver
CMD ["vainfo", "--display", "drm"]
```
which I build and run:
```console
$ podman build --tag test .
$ podman run --userns keep-id --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/dri/renderD128 test
Trying display: drm
error: failed to initialize display 'drm'
```

## Alternatives (also not working)

I have tried a number of other combinations of arguments to `podman`:
- `podman run --userns keep-id --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/dri/renderD128 test`
- `podman run                  --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/dri/renderD128 test`
- `podman run --userns keep-id                         --device /dev/dri/renderD128 test`
- `podman run --userns keep-id --group-add 488         --device /dev/dri/renderD128 test`
- `podman run --userns keep-id --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/dri/           test`
- `podman run --userns keep-id --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/dri/:/dev/dri/ test`

but they all give identical results.

## Details

<details>
<summary>For the record, my `subuid` and `subgid` are:</summary>
```console
$ cat /etc/subuid
matt:100000:65536
root:400000000:500000001
myuser:165536:65536
$ cat /etc/subgid
matt:100000:65536
root:400000000:500000001
myuser:165536:65536
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>and running `id` inside the container gives:</summary>
```console
$ podman run --userns keep-id --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/dri/renderD128 test id
uid=1001(myuser) gid=1001(mygroup) groups=1001(mygroup)
$ podman run --userns keep-id --group-add 488         --device /dev/dri/renderD128 test id
uid=1001(myuser) gid=1001(mygroup) groups=1001(mygroup),488
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>and showing that SELinux is disabled:</summary>
```console
$ sestatus 
SELinux status:                 enabled
SELinuxfs mount:                /sys/fs/selinux
SELinux root directory:         /etc/selinux
Loaded policy name:             targeted
Current mode:                   permissive
Mode from config file:          enforcing
Policy MLS status:              enabled
Policy deny_unknown status:     allowed
Memory protection checking:     actual (secure)
Max kernel policy version:      34
```
</details>

# Other tests

I can test a similar setup with volumes where I create a directory on the host system with similar permissions:

```console
$ ls -hl /var/foo
total 4.0K
-rw-r--r--. 1 root render 5 Sep  6 13:57 bar
$ cat /var/foo/bar 
blah
```
and mounting this as a volume and `cat`ing it in the container works fine:
```console
$ podman run --userns keep-id --group-add keep-groups --volume /var/foo:/foo test cat /foo/bar
blah
```
But trying to mount the device a a volume does not work:
```console
$ podman run --userns keep-id --group-add keep-groups --volume /dev/dri/renderD128 test
Trying display: drm
error: failed to initialize display 'drm'
```

# Hack solution

If I edit the ownership of `/dev/dri/renderD128` to either be owned by the user `myuser` or by the group `mygroup` (the user's primary group) then the `podman run ...` command works.

```console
$ ls -hl /dev/dri/renderD128 
crw-rw----. 1 root mygroup 226, 128 Sep  6 00:53 /dev/dri/renderD128
$ podman run --userns keep-id --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/dri/renderD128 test
Trying display: drm
libva info: VA-API version 1.22.0
...
```

It also works if I build the container and run it as `root` and avoid the `myuser` account entirely.

# Summary

It seems that the secondary groups of the user are not being mapped into the container as I would expect. Or at least the processes inside the container are not gaining the privileges of them. However this only seems to be affecting devices and not normal volume mounts.

It's been a few months, so sorry to shamelessly bump, but did anybody manage to solve the issue? Is there any non-hacky way to actually properly mount the devices (such as `/dev/kfd`) into a rootless-container, where file ownership is sometrhing else than `nobody` and `nogroup`?
Yes, for me, my previously posted solution worked fine, however I don't know what could be wrong with @milliams 's setup

I've now solved it for my use case. The problem was that Podman was using `runc` as the runtime rather than `runc`. It seems that the `--group-add keep-groups` flag is only supported on `crun` (this is documented but I either didn't notice it or didn't understand it since I'm new to Podman).

Now, with a `containers.conf` of:
```toml
[engine]
runtime="crun"
```

I can do `podman run --userns keep-id --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/dri/renderD128 test` (based on my above containers) and it works correctly:
```
libva info: VA-API version 1.22.0
Trying display: drm
libva info: Trying to open /usr/lib/dri/iHD_drv_video.so
libva info: Found init function __vaDriverInit_1_22
libva info: va_openDriver() returns 0
vainfo: VA-API version: 1.22 (libva 2.22.0)
vainfo: Driver version: Intel iHD driver for Intel(R) Gen Graphics - 25.2.6 ()
vainfo: Supported profile and entrypoints
...
```
but I note that the `ls` listing for the device in the container still shows `nobody`:
```bash-session
$ podman run --userns keep-id --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/dri/renderD128 test ls -hl /dev/dri
total 0      
crw-rw----    1 nobody   nobody    226, 128 Dec 19 12:51 renderD128
```
but I don't think that matters.

In summary: use `crun` as the Podman runtime.

you need to chmod the directory you are trying to use like /dev/dri so that rootless podman can read it.  I think 700 may work but I just make it sudo chmod -R 777 /dev/dri and be done with it.  (note, security experts are crying right now)
